name: Mamie Smith
abstract: Mamie Smith (née Robinson; May 26, 1891 – August or September 16, 1946) was an American singer. As a vaudeville singer, she performed in multiple styles, including jazz and blues.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: And so far, he went to prove he was king, he went to Chicago to challenge Lurie. And reputed to have, went to the band, said, say, hand me that horn, boy. Show you how to play that horn. And Lurie handed him the horn, and he played, and when he got through, Lurie got angry, was angry about it, and s

about_quotes.speaker: George P. Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: I used to hear him on the old 78s and I also heard King Oliver and, and on a lot of music because my father used to play the old 78 records. He had a lot of 78 records with blues by Betsy Smith, Mamie Smith, Ida Cox and all these singers.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry "Sweets" Edison
about_quotes.evidence: My mother had music all the time, all kinds of records, you know of Louis Armstrong, Bessie Smith, Mamie Smith, all the blues... she had it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry "Sweets" Edison interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I used to listen to every instrument, you know. ... [INTERVIEWER] It was Mamie Smith I used to listen to, and Bessie Smith...

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Jazz was about all that we heard in Kentucky at that time, the blues, Bessie Smith, Mamie Smith, Blind Lemon Jefferson, Leadbelly…
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...That was exactly what I was trying to say. Well, he'd have his records and these records would be Mamie Smith...
